texts.full_text: wn to the ship when there's a surprise inspection. During the conversation between Aurora and Octavia, Bellamy approached the two. When Bellamy and Aurora got into an argument about his loyalty and him going to Octavia, Aurora stated that she never hid that her child was a product of rape, but Bellamy refused to listen. Before Bellamy and Octavia left, Aurora kissed Bellamy goodbye. During her death in Blood Must Have Blood, Octavia stated that she is her mother's only child. However, there are two things that could be inferred from this statement. First, Octavia and her sister had the same parents. This is likely because Bellamy is Octavia's half-brother, who may not have known that Octavia was his sister when Aurora asked him to name her. Secondly, it could mean that Octavia did not survive childbirth. Bellamy Blake was born to two unknown parents, although they are presumed dead, because Bellamy didn't know anything about them. In His Sister's Keeper, Bellamy watched his mother give birth to his sister and helped keep her quiet. He learned her name was Aurora and his sister's name was Octavia. He also learned how his parents had an extra child and the law they violated as a result. Bellamy was told by his mother Aurora to take his half-sister, Octavia, away from the Ark when she turned sixteen. When she reached the required age, Aurora was ready to help them and take her out of Sector B-17 where she was living. When Bellamy saw the Ark Guards at the drop ship on the day of Octavia's surprise inspection, he went back and informed Aurora and his half-sister what was happening. When the Ark Guard took Aurora to the front of the station after being discovered with an extra child, she told Bellamy to take her daughter away. When he asked where, she simply told him that he'd know where they had to go. Octavia and Aurora Aurora and Octavia's first and last seen together was when Octavia turned

texts.full_text: ave the keys of Death and Hades. Isaiah 43:10 “You are my witnesses,” declares the Lord, “and my servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me. John 8:23-24 He said to them, “You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world. I told you that you would die in your sins, for unless you believe that I am he you will die in your sins.” John 8:58 Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am.” John 1:15 (John bore witness about him, and cried out, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me ranks before me, because he was before me.’”) Luke 21:13 This will be your opportunity to bear witness. Matthew 24:25 See, I have told you beforehand. Matthew 11:3 and said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or shall we look for another?” Malachi 3:1 “Behold, I send my messenger, and he will prepare the way before me. And the Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple; and the messenger of the covenant in whom you delight, behold, he is coming, says the Lord of hosts.
